title: Verbascum deserticola

parts: Verbascum deserticola is a desert mullein that has been used in traditional medicine. intro: Verbascum deserticola, also known as Desert Mullein, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the Scrophulariaceae family, native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The leaves and flowers of Desert Mullein have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to their anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Desert Mullein include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and iridoid glycosides, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Desert Mullein is commonly used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as in topical applications for skin conditions and wounds.
